page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-16 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The instance is configured with 64 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU. The CPU includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports hardware-level features such as AVX, AES, VAES, SHA_NI, BMI2, ADX, and FMA. This configuration does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing the instance in the top 25% of tested hardware. However, multi-core CPU performance falls into the average range, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores. Memory bandwidth is average across both cached small-block reads (1017.84 GB/sec) and uncached large-block reads (70.96 GB/sec) and writes (44.82 GB/sec). Text generation for small language models (135M parameters) shows strong performance at 263.72 tokens/sec, while prompt processing and larger model inference (7B parameters) remain in the average tier.

From a resource density perspective, the n2d-standard-16 offers a standard hardware profile that balances compute and memory without the added cost of specialized accelerators or local storage. The lack of local NVMe SSDs represents a tradeoff for storage-intensive workloads, requiring external block storage. The instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, software compilation, database operations, and moderate text-processing tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-16 is a general-purpose server instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It contains 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core, 32 MB of L3 cache, and lacks local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance and strong text generation speeds for small language models, while multi-core compute, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. The instance provides a balanced resource profile, making it a viable option for standard workloads such as web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose database tasks that do not require local high-speed storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-16 is a general-purpose server with 16 dedicated vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, delivering strong single-core performance for standard workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-16 features 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, offering balanced general-purpose performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, strong single-core performance, and a standard compute-to-memory ratio.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB RAM on AMD EPYC 7B13 architecture.
Strong single-core CPU performance and small-model text generation.
Average multi-core compute, memory bandwidth, and database performance.
No bundled local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators.
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose databases.
